title: Kth Smallest Element in a BST
description: Given a binary search tree (BST), find the kth smallest element in it. You may assume that k is always valid, 1 ≤ k ≤ number of nodes in the BST.

examples.explanation: The smallest element in the BST is 1. Thus, the 1st smallest element is 1.
In-order traversal of the BST gives us [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]. The 3rd smallest element is 3.
In-order traversal of the BST gives us [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]. The 4th smallest element is 4.

constraints: The number of nodes in the BST is n.
hints: Consider performing an in-order traversal of the BST.
Keep track of the count of nodes visited during the traversal.
Once you reach the kth node in the in-order sequence, you've found your answer.
Think about how to optimize the search process without traversing all nodes.
